title: CAPPA-2: Cisplatin Added to Gemcitabine in Poor Performance Advanced NSCLC Patients

eligibility.text: Histologically or cytologically confirmed diagnosis of advanced non small cell lung cancer, not amenable to surgical or curative radiation therapy (stage IIIB or IV);
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the study population is representative of a specific disease group for which treatment efficacy can be assessed.

eligibility.text: No prior or concomitant chemotherapy (adjuvant/neoadjuvant chemotherapy permitted if concluded at least one year prior to enrollment);
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the study population is comparable and avoids confounding factors from previous treatments.
This criterion ensures patients are able to participate in the trial with minimal physical limitations.
This criterion sets a specific age range for patient inclusion, ensuring the study population is manageable.
This criterion ensures patients have sufficient time to participate in the trial and potentially experience treatment effects.

eligibility.text: Normal bone marrow, hepatic and renal function defined as: neutrophils > or = 2000/mm3, PLT > or = 100,000/mm3, Hb > or = 10.0 g/dl, Bilirubin > or = 1.5 times the upper normal limit (UNL), AST and ALT < or = 3 times the UNL (5 times in the presence of liver metastases), creatinine within normal limits;
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients have healthy organ function to minimize risks associated with treatment.
This criterion ensures patient understanding and agreement to participate in the trial.
This criterion ensures patients are free from active infections that could interfere with treatment or data collection.

eligibility.text: Severe concomitant illness (congestive heart failure, angina pectoris, myocardial infarction within previous 6 months, cardiac arrhythmias under treatment, severe arterial hypertension, severe or uncontrolled diabetes mellitus);
eligibility.justification: This criterion excludes patients with conditions that could increase the risk of adverse events or complications from treatment.
This criterion ensures patients have adequate organ function to tolerate potential side effects of the study drug.

eligibility.text: Radiation therapy ongoing or concluded within two weeks prior to enrollment;
eligibility.justification: This criterion excludes patients who are undergoing active radiation treatment, which could interfere with the study's primary endpoint.
This criterion excludes patients with symptomatic brain metastases, potentially impacting their ability to participate in the trial.
This criterion ensures a clear comparison between treatment groups and avoids confounding factors from prior treatments.

eligibility.text: Any condition that would, in the investigator's opinion, limit the patients ability to provide informed consent or to comply with study procedures;
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patient safety and compliance by excluding individuals who may not be able to fully participate.
This criterion excludes pregnant or breastfeeding women, as the study drug could potentially harm the fetus or infant.

eligibility.text: Any malignancy within the past 5 years (except for adequately treated carcinoma in situ of the cervix or non melanoma skin cancer);
eligibility.justification: This criterion excludes patients with a history of cancer, potentially impacting their ability to participate in the trial.

endpoints.timeframe: each cycle of chemotherapy and every 3 months thereafter

adaptive_designs.description: Standard fixed-design trial. No adaptive features detected.

safety_adverse_events.ae_ascertainment: Not explicitly described (assumed standard investigator reporting)
safety_adverse_events.safety_monitoring: Investigator-reported with sponsor oversight (no DSMB mentioned)

pharmacogenetics.summary: No known pharmacogenetic drug classes or genes detected in protocol text.
